author: reviewer-g1
text: The algorithm keeps recycling my own taste back at me. Total echo chamber — I already have and already follow these artists.

author: reviewer-g2
text: Discover Weekly has gone stale and repetitive. I never discover anything genuinely new from it anymore.

author: reviewer-g3
text: I'm a power user who digs deep cuts and obscure artists below a certain popularity. Spotify's recommendations and discovery are far too cautious and safe for me.

author: reviewer-g4
text: Two kids, zero energy after work to look for music. I just fall back on the same comfort playlist on repeat.

author: reviewer-g5
text: High intent here — I open Discover Weekly, save a couple, then forget them. Nothing ever converts into a habit.

author: reviewer-g6
text: Overwhelmed by the firehose of new releases every Friday. Discovery is just too much effort and too high-effort to keep up with.

author: reviewer-g7
text: Please add a comfort-to-curiosity control. Let me push the algorithm to be more out there and adventurous when I want.

author: reviewer-g8
text: Recommendations arrive with no context. Just tell me why this track fits my taste so I can trust it.

author: reviewer-g9
text: I want to get into classical but every playlist is a 5-hour firehose. A staged, guided on-ramp would actually help a beginner.

author: reviewer-g10
text: New music never sticks. I play something once and it's gone — it never enters my long-term rotation.
